finding_results.notes: The post-call stretch now lands the murder turn once in the immediate aftermath of Joel's call, then advances through new external beats: the men's changed placement, the already-contaminated scene, Nate choosing the dock beside Doug, the charged wait, and Mallick's arrival.

Tommy's entrance now gives Doug concrete anomalies without overclaiming prior knowledge: he arrives without asking a question, stops short of the wet planks, reacts visibly to the word 'held,' drops his light, and Doug limits himself to unease rather than certainty.
The dock-walk memory keeps Martin noticing Doug's decline but removes the repeated inventory of weight loss and gauntness.
Tommy is no longer reintroduced through height, build, crew cut, hands, or scar. The scene now relies on silhouette, movement, and reaction.
The adjacent-chapter redundancy was trimmed. The chapter now uses only glancing draft-board references and no longer restates wall placement or the red-circled picks.
A full narration pass removed expository and appositional em dashes. Only two em-dash lines remain, both genuine interrupted-speech beats.

summary: Revised Chapter 14 to make the murder turn land once and keep generating fresh consequence, recalibrated Tommy's reaction to observable unease instead of author-certified guilt, removed nearby cross-chapter redundancies, and brought em-dash and 'He' opener density under the packet thresholds.
